skill.name: wap-ingestion
skill.description: Guide safe S3 data ingestion into Bauplan with the Write-Audit-Publish pattern, temporary branches, row-count checks, and controlled publish steps.
skill.summary: Load S3 data into Bauplan through a branch-based Write-Audit-Publish workflow.
skill.author: BauplanLabs

security_audit.summary: The high weak-cryptography alerts are false positives, and most backtick alerts are Markdown inline code or examples, not Ruby execution. The skill has legitimate elevated operational risk because it enables Bash-driven ingestion workflows, networked Bauplan and S3 operations, and optional publishing to main. Human review should note the unvalidated SQL identifier construction and assert-based safety checks in the template.

medium_findings.title: Optional Publish Step Can Modify Main Data Branch
medium_findings.description: The skill supports automatic merge to main and documents manual CLI merge commands. This is legitimate for Write-Audit-Publish, but mistakes can publish bad data if review is skipped.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The merge behavior is explicitly documented and implemented. It is not malicious, but it can affect production data when selected.

medium_findings.title: Unvalidated SQL Identifier Construction
medium_findings.description: The template builds a SQL row-count query from namespace and table name without validation or identifier quoting. A crafted table name could break the audit query or alter the intended SQL.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The f-string directly inserts table identifiers into SQL. The exact Bauplan SQL execution impact depends on backend parsing and permissions.

medium_findings.title: Safety Checks Rely on Python Assertions
medium_findings.description: The template uses assert statements for branch existence, table existence, and non-empty import checks. These checks can be disabled when Python runs with optimization enabled.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The assertions are visible in the template and guard safety-critical workflow steps. The risk is conditional on optimized Python execution.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Alerts Are Mostly False Positives
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged many backticks as Ruby or shell execution. Most locations are Markdown inline code, method names, or fenced examples rather than executable skill code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line review shows Markdown formatting and documentation examples. The actual template uses the Bauplan SDK rather than Ruby backtick execution.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Documentation URL Is Benign
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL points to Bauplan documentation and matches the allowed WebFetch domain. No evidence found that data or credentials are sent to that URL.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL is a documentation reference and the allowed tool restricts access to the same domain. There is no evidence of exfiltration behavior.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static weak-cryptography alerts at the reported lines do not correspond to cryptographic code. No hash, cipher, or password handling operation is present there.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain descriptive text and a Markdown table header. I did not find evidence of weak cryptographic algorithms at those locations.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bash Tool and CLI Merge Workflow
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill enables Bash and documents commands that run Python ingestion scripts and merge branches. This is expected for the workflow but should be treated as an external command capability.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The allowed tool and documented commands are explicit. They are operationally sensitive but not evidence of malicious intent.

dangerous_patterns.title: Networked Data Import Through Bauplan SDK
dangerous_patterns.description: The template imports data from user-provided S3 paths into Bauplan. This network access is central to the skill and must use authorized buckets and credentials.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The skill explicitly requests an S3 URI and the template passes it to create_table and import_data. The behavior is intended, but it is still networked data movement.

dangerous_patterns.title: Dynamic SQL String Formatting
dangerous_patterns.description: The audit query uses formatted namespace and table values in SQL. Identifier validation should be added before publishing this as a safe template.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The SQL f-string is directly visible and depends on caller-supplied identifiers. Impact depends on backend SQL parsing and permissions.

content.user_title: Ingest S3 Data Safely with WAP
content.value_statement: Loading data directly into production tables can create errors that are hard to reverse. This skill creates a branch-based Bauplan workflow that writes, audits, and publishes data with review controls.
actual_capabilities: Generates a Bauplan Python ingestion script from a WAP template.
Asks for the required S3 path and target table before creating the script.
Creates a temporary branch before importing data into Bauplan.
Runs a row-count audit to confirm that data was imported.
Supports inspection-first and automatic merge publish modes.
Documents manual CLI merge steps after user inspection.
limitations: Requires valid Bauplan credentials and access to the target S3 data.
Only includes a basic row-count audit by default.
Does not validate table names or namespace values in the provided template.
Automatic merge can publish bad data if users skip review.

use_cases.title: Load New Analytics Tables
use_cases.description: Create a new Bauplan table from S3 files while keeping production unchanged until review.

use_cases.title: Review Imports Before Publish
use_cases.description: Keep an ingestion branch open so row counts and sample queries can be checked before merge.

use_cases.title: Automate Repeatable S3 Ingestion
use_cases.description: Build a reusable script for controlled S3 imports with consistent branch cleanup behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Safe Import
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new table.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me ingest S3 data into Bauplan using WAP. Ask me for the S3 path, table name, and review preferences.

prompt_templates.title: Create an Inspection Workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Keep the branch open after import.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a WAP ingestion script for my S3 dataset and keep the branch open after a successful row-count audit.

prompt_templates.title: Append to an Existing Table
prompt_templates.scenario: Load new files without recreating a table.
prompt_templates.prompt: Adapt the WAP workflow to append S3 data to an existing Bauplan table and preserve the branch for inspection.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a Reviewed Publish
prompt_templates.prompt: After I inspect the WAP branch, guide me through publishing it to main and cleaning up the branch safely.

output_examples.input: A user provides an S3 parquet path, table name orders, and wants inspection before merge.
output_examples.output: The assistant prepares a Bauplan WAP script, leaves the temporary branch open, and reports the branch name for review.

output_examples.input: A user asks to append daily files to an existing transactions table.
output_examples.output: The assistant adjusts the workflow to import into the existing table on a branch and run the row-count audit.

output_examples.input: A user has inspected the branch and wants to publish it.
output_examples.output: The assistant confirms the target branch, switches to main, merges the WAP branch, and explains cleanup steps.

best_practices: Use inspection mode unless the ingestion path and table are already trusted.
Add domain-specific quality checks before merging a branch to main.
Validate table names, namespace values, and S3 paths before running the script.
anti_patterns: Do not auto-merge unfamiliar data without inspecting row counts and sample records.
Do not use broad S3 wildcards unless the matched files are well understood.
Do not treat the default row-count audit as complete data validation.

faq.question: What does WAP mean?
faq.answer: WAP means Write-Audit-Publish. Data is written to a branch, checked, then published only after approval.

faq.question: Does this skill run command-line tools?
faq.answer: Yes. It can run Python scripts and documents Bauplan CLI merge steps after inspection.

faq.question: Can it merge data into main automatically?
faq.answer: Yes. The merge option can publish to main, but inspection mode is the safer default.

faq.question: What data formats are supported?
faq.answer: The skill is designed for S3 sources such as parquet, csv, and jsonl files supported by Bauplan.

faq.question: Does it perform full data quality validation?
faq.answer: No. The template checks that rows were imported. Add business-specific checks before publish.

faq.question: What credentials are required?
faq.answer: You need Bauplan access and permission to read the S3 source path used for ingestion.
